info.title: Plesk RESTful API
info.description: New remote API for Plesk is based on REST concepts and provides the output in JSON format.

tags.name: Databases
tags.description: Databases, database servers and database users entry points

post.description: Create a new API secret key for authentication with specified login credentials and IP restrictions.

delete.description: Revoke and delete an API secret key, removing all associated access permissions.

post.description:
Create a new session for the specified user login. Returns a session identifier
that can be used to access Plesk panel via the URL:

    https://<host>:<port>/enterprise/rsession_init.php?PLESKSESSID=<id>

get.description: Retrieve a list of all available Plesk CLI commands that can be executed via the REST API.
Retrieve detailed reference information for a specific Plesk CLI command, including allowed subcommands and options.

post.description: Execute a Plesk CLI command (a Plesk utility, e.g. one run via `plesk bin <utility>` or `/usr/local/psa/bin/<utility>`) with the provided parameters and environment. Set `fail_on_error` to true so that a failed command is reported as an error (HTTP 422) instead of a 200 response with a non-zero exit code.

422.description: Respond with 422 status when the command returns a non-zero exit code.

get.description: Retrieve server metadata including Plesk version, platform, and system information.

post.description: Perform initial server configuration, including admin credentials and basic settings.

200.description: Inital server setup was succesfully performed

post.description: Install or update the Plesk or additional license key for the server.

get.description: Retrieve a list of all IP addresses configured on the server.
Retrieve a list of all Plesk extensions currently installed on the server.

post.description: Install a Plesk extension from the Extension Catalog by ID, a file, or URL.

get.description: Retrieve detailed information about a specific installed extension.

delete.description: Remove an extension from the server and delete all associated data.

put.description: Enable a previously disabled extension, making it active on the server.
Disable an extension temporarily without uninstalling it from the server.

get.description: Retrieve a list of all client accounts on the server that are available to the user.

post.description: Create a new client account with the specified settings and permissions.

get.description: Retrieve detailed information about a specific client account.

put.description: Update settings and properties of an existing client account.

delete.description: Delete a client account and all associated data from the server.

get.description: Retrieve all domains associated with a specific client account.
Retrieve resource usage statistics for a specific client account.

put.description: Activate a suspended client account, restoring access to all services.
Suspend a client account, temporarily blocking access to all services.

get.description: Retrieve a list of domains available to the user, with optional filtering by domain name.

post.description: Create a new domain with the specified configuration and hosting settings. To create an addon domain, provide `base_domain`. To create a subdomain, provide `parent_domain`. To assign the domain to a specific client, provide `owner_client`; if omitted, the domain will be assigned to the currently logged-in user.

get.description: Retrieve detailed information about a specific domain.

put.description: Update settings and configuration for an existing domain.

delete.description: Delete a domain and all associated data from the server.

get.description: Retrieve information about the client account that owns the domain.
Retrieve the current operational status of a domain (active, suspended, disabled).

put.description: Change the operational status of a domain (enable, suspend, or disable).

post.summary: Execute a shell command on a domain
post.description: Execute a command in the context of a domain, running as the subscription's system user with the domain's document root as the default working directory, and retrieve its stdout, stderr and exit code. Works even if the system user has no or limited shell access, and allows operations on the domain content without root privileges. Set `fail_on_error` to true so that a failed command is reported as an error (HTTP 422) instead of a 200 response with a non-zero exit code. For pipes, redirections or other shell features, wrap the command, e.g. bash -lc '<your commands>'.

422.description: The command exited with a non-zero code (returned only when fail_on_error is enabled). The command output is still included in the body.

get.summary: Run a DNS resolution check for a domain
get.description:
Runs a DNS troubleshooting report for the domain, returning a list of checks
(DNS management, web, mail, email authentication, propagation) with severity,
findings, and recommended actions, plus an aggregate summary.

500.description: Failed to execute the backend DNS check utility

501.description: DNS resolution check is available in Plesk version >= 18.0.80

get.description:
Search domain logs (Apache error logs, Nginx error logs, PHP-FPM error logs, HTTP access logs, FTP xferlog, ModSecurity audit logs) for a pattern and return matching entries with log statistics grouped by log type.
Only available on Linux-based Plesk servers.

parameters.name: pattern
parameters.description:
Search pattern (regex) to filter log entries.

**Security restrictions**: Patterns with nested or adjacent quantifiers are rejected to prevent ReDoS attacks.
Examples of invalid patterns: `(a+)+`, `a*+`, `(error)+*`

Keep patterns simple and specific. Use character classes instead of alternation where possible.

parameters.name: start_time
parameters.description: Start time for log search in RFC3339 format (e.g., 2026-05-21T14:30:00Z)

parameters.name: end_time
parameters.description: End time for log search in RFC3339 format (e.g., 2026-05-21T14:30:00Z). Must be >= start_time

parameters.name: sample_size
parameters.description: Maximum number of sample log entries to return

get.description: Return stat metadata for a single file or directory. The default path is the domain's document root.

parameters.name: path

403.description: Access to the specified domain is denied, or path is outside the webspace.

404.description: Domain not found, or path does not exist.

delete.description: Delete a file or directory at the given path. Directories are deleted recursively.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description:
Path to delete. An absolute path (starting with "/" on Unix, or a drive letter such as "C:\\" on Windows) is used as-is;
otherwise the path is resolved relative to the domain's document root.

403.description: Access to the specified domain is denied, or path is outside the webspace.

404.description: Domain not found, or path does not exist.

get.description: List files and directories at the specified path. By default the listing is rooted at the domain's document root.

parameters.name: path

403.description: Access to the specified domain is denied, or path is outside the webspace.

404.description: Domain not found or path is not a directory.

get.description:
Download the contents of a regular file. Without `encoding`, the response is a raw octet-stream
attachment. When `encoding` is specified, the response is a JSON body with the file contents
encoded accordingly. Files larger than 100 MB cannot be downloaded via the JSON mode.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description:
Target file path. An absolute path (starting with "/" on Unix, or a drive letter such as "C:\\" on Windows) is used as-is;
otherwise the path is resolved relative to the domain's document root.

parameters.name: encoding
parameters.description: When specified, returns a `FileContentResponse` JSON body instead of an octet-stream.

403.description: Access to the specified domain is denied, or path is outside the webspace.

404.description: Domain not found, or path does not exist.

put.description:
Upload the request body as a file at the given path. Creates the file if it does not exist; overwrites it if it does.

When sending `Content-Type: application/octet-stream`, the raw request body is written verbatim.
When sending `Content-Type: application/json`, the body must be a `FileContentRequest` object.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description:
Destination file path. An absolute path (starting with "/" on Unix, or a drive letter such as "C:\\" on Windows) is used as-is;
otherwise the path is resolved relative to the domain's document root.

403.description: Access to the specified domain is denied, or path is outside the webspace.

patch.summary: Append a chunk to a file
patch.description:
Append the request body to the end of a file at the given path. Creates the file if it does not exist.

When sending `Content-Type: application/octet-stream`, the raw request body is appended verbatim.
When sending `Content-Type: application/json`, the body must be a `FileContentRequest` object.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description:
Destination file path. An absolute path (starting with "/" on Unix, or a drive letter such as "C:\\" on Windows) is used as-is;
otherwise the path is resolved relative to the domain's document root.

403.description: Access to the specified domain is denied, or path is outside the webspace.

post.description: Create a new directory within the domain's webspace.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description:
Path of the directory to create. An absolute path (starting with "/" on Unix, or a drive letter such as "C:\\" on Windows) is used as-is;
otherwise the path is resolved relative to the domain's document root.

403.description: Access to the specified domain is denied, or path is outside the webspace.

post.description: Copy a file or directory from source to destination within the domain's webspace. Returns stat metadata of the resulting entry at the destination path.

403.description: Access to the specified domain is denied, or a path is outside the webspace.

404.description: Domain not found, or source path does not exist.

409.description: Destination already exists and overwrite is false.

post.summary: Move or rename a file or directory
post.description: Move (rename) a file or directory from source to destination within the domain's webspace. Returns stat metadata of the resulting entry at the destination path.

403.description: Access to the specified domain is denied, or a path is outside the webspace.

404.description: Domain not found, or source path does not exist.

409.description: Destination already exists and overwrite is false.

put.description: Set the permission mode (chmod) of a file or directory within the domain's webspace. Has no effect on Windows. Returns the updated stat metadata.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Path to the file or directory. An absolute path (starting with "/" on Unix, or a drive letter such as "C:\" on Windows) is used as-is; otherwise relative to the document root.

403.description: Access to the specified domain is denied, or the path is outside the webspace.

404.description: Domain not found, or the path does not exist.

get.description: Retrieve a list of FTP users with optional filtering by username or domain.

post.description: Create a new FTP user account with specified permissions and home directory.

put.description: Update FTP user settings such as password, home directory, or permissions.

delete.description: Delete an FTP user account and revoke all associated access.

get.description: Retrieve a list of all databases on the server, with optional filtering by domain name.

post.description: Create a new database on the specified database server.

delete.description: Delete a database and all its data from the database server.

get.description: Retrieve a list of database users, with optional filtering by database ID.

post.description: Create a new database user with specified credentials and database association.

put.description: Update database user settings, such as password or access permissions.

delete.description: Delete a database user and revoke all associated access rights.

get.description: Retrieve a list of all database servers configured on the system.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Filter data by database server id

get.description: Retrieve all DNS records for a specific domain or domain alias.

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: Filter by domain or domain alias name

404.description: Can not find DNS record by id '123'

post.description: Add a new DNS record to a domain or domain alias zone.

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: Filter by domain or domain alias name

404.description: Domain or Domain Alias is not found

get.description: Retrieve details of a specific DNS record by its ID.

put.description: Modify an existing DNS record's properties and values.

delete.description: Remove a DNS record from the domain's DNS zone.

ImpersonateLoginHeader.name: X-Impersonate-Login
ImpersonateLoginHeader.description: Allows admin users to impersonate another user account. The API request will be executed in the context of the specified user. Only administrators can use this header.

id.description: Entity with the specified ID successfully created

guid.description: Entity successfully created, new GUID is

message.example: Account with this name already exists

ip.description: The IP address that will be linked to the key. If this node or 'ips' node is not specified, the IP address of the request sender will be used.

ips.description: Array of IP addresses that will be linked to the key. If this node or 'ip' node is not specified, the IP address of the request sender will be used.

login.description: The login name of an existing customer or a reseller that will have this secret key. The customer's or reseller's account should be active. If this node is not specified, the administrator's login will be used.
The login name of the user to create a session for.

user_ip.description: The IP address of the user who will use the session. If not specified, the IP address of the request sender will be used.

skip_mfa.description: Skip multi-factor authentication check for the created session.

id.description: The session identifier. Use it to access the Plesk panel via: https://<host>:<port>/enterprise/rsession_init.php?PLESKSESSID=<id>

fail_on_error.description: Respond with 422 status when the command returns a non-zero exit code. Recommended to enable when executing commands so failures are reported as errors instead of a successful response.

password.description: The new Plesk Administrator's password that will replace the default one.

city.description: The name of the city where administrator lives.

state.description: The name of state (for US citizens) or province where administrator lives.

country.description: The name of the country where administrator lives.

send_announce.description: It specifies whether Plesk announcement messages are sent to the administrator's e-mail.

locale.description: The locale used by the administrator.

multiple_sessions.description: It specifies the information if the administrator has multiple sessions in Plesk.

ipv4.description: The IP address in Plesk database (IPv4).

ipv6.description: The IP address in Plesk database (IPv6).

type.description: The type of IP address. Allowed values: shared | exclusive.

additional.description: It specifies if the key that will be installed is an additional key.

name.description: Client's personal name (1 to 60 characters long).

company.description: The company name (0 to 60 characters long).

login.description: The login name of the client account (1 to 60 characters long).

status.description: The current status of the client account. Allowed values: 0 (active) | 16 (disabled by admin) | 4 (under backup/restore) | 256 (expired).

email.description: The email address of the client account owner (0 to 255 characters long).

locale.description: The locale used on the client account. Default value: en-US.

guid.description: The global user ID of the client account just added to Plesk.

owner_login.description: The login name of a client account owner. If the client account owner is Plesk Administrator, specify the admin login name.

external_id.description: The client GUID in the Plesk components (for example, Business Manager).

description.description: The description for the client account.

type.description: The type of the client account. Allowed values: reseller | customer | admin.

name.description: Client's personal name (1 to 60 characters long).

company.description: The company name (0 to 60 characters long).

login.description: The login name of the client account (1 to 60 characters long).

status.description: The current status of the client account. Allowed values: 0 (active) | 16 (disabled by admin) | 4 (under backup/restore) | 256 (expired).

email.description: The email address of the client account owner (0 to 255 characters long).

locale.description: The locale used on the client account. Default value: en-US.

owner_login.description: The login name of a client account owner. If the client account owner is Plesk Administrator, specify the admin login name.

external_id.description: The client GUID in the Plesk components (for example, Business Manager).

description.description: The description for the client account.

password.description: The password of the client account (5 to 14 characters long).

type.description: The type of the client account. Allowed values: reseller | customer | admin.

name.description: Client's personal name (1 to 60 characters long).

company.description: The company name (0 to 60 characters long).

login.description: The login name of the client account (1 to 60 characters long).

status.description: The current status of the client account. Allowed values: 0 (active) | 16 (disabled by admin) | 4 (under backup/restore) | 256 (expired).

email.description: The email address of the client account owner (0 to 255 characters long).

locale.description: The locale used on the client account. Default value: en-US.

owner_login.description: The login name of a client account owner. If the client account owner is Plesk Administrator, specify the admin login name.

external_id.description: The client GUID in the Plesk components (for example, Business Manager).

description.description: The description for the client account.

password.description: The password of the client account (5 to 14 characters long).

active_domains.description: The number of active domains created by a client.

subdomains.description: The number of sub-domains created by a client.

disk_space.description: The amount of disk space occupied by the given a client.

email_postboxes.description: The number of email boxes created by a client.

email_redirects.description: The number of redirects created by a client.

email_response_messages.description: The number of automatic response messages created by a client.

mailing_lists.description: The number of mailing lists created by a client.

databases.description: The number of databases used by a client.

traffic.description: The amount of traffic (in bytes) spent by a client monthly.

traffic_prevday.description: The amount of traffic (in bytes) spent by a client during the previous day.

hosting_type.description:
It specifies the hosting type of the created domain. Allowed values: `virtual` | `standard_forwarding` | `frame_forwarding` | `none`.
Meanings: `virtual` - the domain ships with physical hosting.
`standard_forwarding` - the domain ships with standard forwarding.  When the user goes to a domain on which the standard forwarding is set, Plesk redirects this user from the requested URL to the destination URL. This is done explicitly: the user sees the real ‘destination’ address in the path bar of the browser.
`frame_forwarding` - the domain ships with frame forwarding. When the user goes to such site on which frame forwarding is set, Plesk redirects this user from the requested URL to the ‘destination’ URL implicitly (the user still sees the initial URL in the path bar of the browser).
`none` - no hosting ships with the domain.

www_root.description: WWW root directory for the domain (optional).

ip_addresses.description: The IP address associated with the domain.

aliases.description: Domain aliases that point to this domain.

DomainReference.description: Reference to a domain. At least one of id, name, or guid must be provided.

www_root.description: WWW root directory for the domain (optional).

ip_addresses.description: The IP address associated with the domain.

command.description: Command to execute with its arguments. First element is the command name, subsequent elements are arguments.

stdin.description: Standard input to pass to the command.

env.description: Environment variables to set for the command execution.

working_directory.description: Working directory for the command. Defaults to the domain's document root.

fail_on_error.description: Respond with 422 status when the command returns a non-zero exit code. Recommended to enable when executing commands so failures are reported as errors instead of a successful response.

LogTypeInfo.description: Log type information with count and time range

count.description: Number of log entries found for this log type

time_range.description: Time range specific to this log type

min.description: Earliest timestamp for this log type, null if no logs found

max.description: Latest timestamp for this log type, null if no logs found

DomainLogsResponse.description: Domain logs search response with per-log-type time ranges

entries.description: Array of sample log entries matching the search criteria

log_type_counts.description: Log entry counts grouped by log type with individual time ranges

context.example: 203.0.113.5 serves IPv4 visitors, not this server.

evidence.description: Free-form, check-specific supporting data (records, TTL, resolver, timestamp).

summary.description: Neutral counts per result; no overall verdict.

OwnerClientReference.description: Reference to a client. One of the following properties is required.

login.description: The login name of the client account owner.

guid.description: The global user ID of the client account just added to Plesk.

external_id.description: The client GUID in the Plesk components (for example, Business Manager).

PlanReference.description: Identifies the service plan. One of the following properties is required.

home.description: Subdirectory of the WWW Root that user is restricted to

quota.description: Hard disk quota in bytes (if supported by platform)

permissions.description: Access permissions of the user (if supported by platform)

home.description: Subdirectory of the WWW Root that user is restricted to

quota.description: Hard disk quota in bytes (if supported by platform)

permissions.description: Access permissions of the user (if supported by platform)

home.description: Subdirectory of the WWW Root that user is restricted to

quota.description: Hard disk quota in bytes (if supported by platform)

permissions.description: Access permissions of the user (if supported by platform)

default_user_id.description: ID of the default database user for database

is_default.description: Flag that server is default for servers of this type

is_local.description: Flag that server is installed on local service node

database_id.description: ID of the database this user has access to. A value of 0 means the user has access to all databases on the subscription (created with server_id instead of database_id).

DatabaseUserRequest.description: Either parent_domain and server_id together, or database_id must be provided.

server_id.description: ID of the database server (if user should have access to all databases on subscription)

database_id.description: ID of the database (if user should have access to one database)

id.description: Unique identifier of the DNS record

type.description: The type of the DNS record

host.description: The IP address or name of a host, that will be used by DNS

value.description: The value that will be linked with the host value

opt.description: Optional information about the DNS record

ttl.description: The amount of time (in seconds) that slave DNS servers should store the record in a cache

type.description: The type of the DNS record

host.description: The IP address or name of a host, that will be used by DNS

value.description: The value that will be linked with the host value

opt.description: Optional information about the DNS record

ttl.description: The amount of time (in seconds) that slave DNS servers should store the record in a cache

encoding.description: Content transfer encoding of the "content" field.

mode.description: Octal permission mode (4 digits, e.g. "0755"). Only the low nine permission bits (owner/group/other) are accepted; the leading digit must be "0". Setuid, setgid, and sticky bits are not allowed.

source.description:
Source path. A leading "/" is treated as an absolute filesystem path;
otherwise the path is resolved relative to the domain's document root.

destination.description:
Destination path. A leading "/" is treated as an absolute filesystem path;
otherwise the path is resolved relative to the domain's document root.

overwrite.description: Whether to overwrite the destination if it already exists.

encoding.description: Content transfer encoding of the "content" field.

content.description: File contents encoded according to "encoding". May be empty to write an empty file.

path.description: Path relative to the domain's document root. Can be sent back as the `path` query parameter.

mtime.description: Last modification time as Unix timestamp.
